1	package bead
2	
3	// CI guard tests for state-machine drift prevention.
4	//
5	// These four tests fail loudly the moment the persisted-status enumeration
6	// drifts between its four sources of truth:
7	//
8	//  1. TestSchemaEnumMatchesCanonicalStatuses — the JSON Schema enum at
9	//     cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json must equal the
10	//     exported CanonicalStatuses list in types.go (set equality).
11	//  2. TestStatusLiteralAudit — every persisted-status assignment to a
12	//     bead.Bead.Status field anywhere under cli/internal/ must use a
13	//     literal in CanonicalStatuses (or, preferably, the typed StatusXxx
14	//     constant). Catches drift like writing "needs_investigation" as a
15	//     status string instead of using a label.
16	//  3. TestTDDocMatchesSchemaEnum — the persisted-status enumeration block
17	//     in docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
18	//     §2 must equal the schema enum. Forces TD amendment when schema
19	//     changes.
20	//  4. TestSchemaCompatRoundTripPreservesLabelsAndEvents — a bd-export
21	//     fixture under cli/internal/bead/testdata/ must round-trip through
22	//     unmarshalBead → marshalBead → unmarshalBead with all known fields,
23	//     labels, dependencies, and events (in Extra) byte-equivalent. This
24	//     is the ADR-004 bd/br interchange compatibility guard.
25	//
26	// Each guard's failure message points at the exact mismatch location. All
27	// four together run in well under five seconds; CI invokes them through
28	// the standard `go test ./...` pass — no opt-in flag is required.
29	
30	import (
31		"encoding/json"
32		"go/ast"
33		"go/token"
34		"os"
35		"path/filepath"
36		"reflect"
37		"regexp"
38		"runtime"
39		"sort"
40		"strconv"
41		"strings"
42		"testing"
43		"time"
44	
45		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
46		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
47		"golang.org/x/tools/go/packages"
48	)
49	
50	// repoRoot returns the repository root path computed from this test file's
51	// location (cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go → repo root is three levels up).
52	func repoRoot(t *testing.T) string {
53		t.Helper()
54		_, file, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
55		require.True(t, ok, "runtime.Caller failed")
56		// cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go → repo root
57		root, err := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "..", "..", ".."))
58		require.NoError(t, err)
59		return root
60	}
61	
62	// cliDir returns the cli/ directory path (one level under the repo root).
63	func cliDir(t *testing.T) string {
64		return filepath.Join(repoRoot(t), "cli")
65	}
66	
67	// schemaEnum reads the persisted-status enum out of the bead-record JSON
68	// schema. Returned as a sorted, deduplicated slice for stable comparison.
69	func schemaEnum(t *testing.T) []string {
70		t.Helper()
71		path := filepath.Join(cliDir(t), "internal", "bead", "schema", "bead-record.schema.json")
72		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
73		require.NoError(t, err, "read schema at %s", path)
74	
75		var doc struct {
76			Properties struct {
77				Status struct {
78					Enum []string `json:"enum"`
79				} `json:"status"`
80			} `json:"properties"`
81		}
82		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(data, &doc))
83		require.NotEmpty(t, doc.Properties.Status.Enum, "schema status.enum is empty at %s", path)
84		out := append([]string(nil), doc.Properties.Status.Enum...)
85		sort.Strings(out)
86		return out
87	}
88	
89	// canonicalSorted returns CanonicalStatuses as a sorted, deduplicated copy.
90	func canonicalSorted() []string {
91		out := append([]string(nil), CanonicalStatuses...)
92		sort.Strings(out)
93		return out
94	}
95	
96	// TestSchemaEnumMatchesCanonicalStatuses guards the bead-record JSON Schema
97	// status enum against drift from the exported CanonicalStatuses list in
98	// types.go. They must contain exactly the same set of strings.
99	func TestSchemaEnumMatchesCanonicalStatuses(t *testing.T) {
100		got := schemaEnum(t)
101		want := canonicalSorted()
102		if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, want) {
103			t.Fatalf(`schema/bead-record.schema.json status enum is out of sync with bead.CanonicalStatuses.
104	  schema enum (cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json): %v
105	  Go constants (cli/internal/bead/types.go CanonicalStatuses):    %v
106	Update both sides together — adding a new persisted status also requires upstream bd/br coordination and an ADR-004 amendment.`, got, want)
107		}
108	}
109	
110	// statusLiteralFinding records a single offending literal location so the
111	// failure message can point straight at the file and line.
112	type statusLiteralFinding struct {
113		Pos     token.Position
114		Literal string
115		Context string // "assignment" or "composite literal"
116	}
117	
118	// TestStatusLiteralAudit walks every Go source file under cli/internal/
119	// (using go/packages with full type info) and flags any string literal
120	// assigned to a bead.Bead.Status field whose value is not in
121	// CanonicalStatuses. The intent: future code must never write a non-canonical
122	// string into a persisted bead status. The typed StatusXxx constants are the
123	// preferred form, but raw literals that match the canonical set are tolerated
124	// (legacy compatibility); literals outside the set are a hard error.
125	func TestStatusLiteralAudit(t *testing.T) {
126		cfg := &packages.Config{
127			Mode: packages.NeedName | packages.NeedFiles | packages.NeedCompiledGoFiles |
128				packages.NeedImports | packages.NeedDeps | packages.NeedTypes |
129				packages.NeedSyntax | packages.NeedTypesInfo,
130			Dir:   cliDir(t),
131			Tests: false,
132		}
133		pkgs, err := packages.Load(cfg, "./internal/...")
134		require.NoError(t, err)
135		require.NotEmpty(t, pkgs, "no packages loaded under cli/internal/...")
136	
137		canonical := make(map[string]bool, len(CanonicalStatuses))
138		for _, s := range CanonicalStatuses {
139			canonical[s] = true
140		}
141	
142		var findings []statusLiteralFinding
143	
144		for _, pkg := range pkgs {
145			if pkg.TypesInfo == nil {
146				continue
147			}
148			for i, file := range pkg.Syntax {
149				fname := ""
150				if i < len(pkg.CompiledGoFiles) {
151					fname = pkg.CompiledGoFiles[i]
152				}
153				// Skip generated and test files (test files are excluded by
154				// Tests:false above, but be defensive about codegen output).
155				if strings.HasSuffix(fname, "_gen.go") || strings.Contains(fname, "/generated/") {
156					continue
157				}
158				ast.Inspect(file, func(n ast.Node) bool {
159					switch x := n.(type) {
160					case *ast.AssignStmt:
161						for li, lhs := range x.Lhs {
162							sel, ok := lhs.(*ast.SelectorExpr)
163							if !ok || sel.Sel == nil || sel.Sel.Name != "Status" {
164								continue
165							}
166							if !receiverIsBead(pkg, sel.X) {
167								continue
168							}
169							if li >= len(x.Rhs) {
170								continue
171							}
172							lit, ok := x.Rhs[li].(*ast.BasicLit)
173							if !ok || lit.Kind != token.STRING {
174								continue
175							}
176							val, err := strconv.Unquote(lit.Value)
177							if err != nil {
178								continue
179							}
180							if !canonical[val] {
181								findings = append(findings, statusLiteralFinding{
182									Pos:     pkg.Fset.Position(lit.Pos()),
183									Literal: val,
184									Context: "assignment to bead.Bead.Status",
185								})
186							}
187						}
188					case *ast.CompositeLit:
189						if !compositeIsBead(pkg, x) {
190							return true
191						}
192						for _, elt := range x.Elts {
193							kv, ok := elt.(*ast.KeyValueExpr)
194							if !ok {
195								continue
196							}
197							key, ok := kv.Key.(*ast.Ident)
198							if !ok || key.Name != "Status" {
199								continue
200							}
201							lit, ok := kv.Value.(*ast.BasicLit)
202							if !ok || lit.Kind != token.STRING {
203								continue
204							}
205							val, err := strconv.Unquote(lit.Value)
206							if err != nil {
207								continue
208							}
209							if !canonical[val] {
210								findings = append(findings, statusLiteralFinding{
211									Pos:     pkg.Fset.Position(lit.Pos()),
212									Literal: val,
213									Context: "composite literal bead.Bead{Status: ...}",
214								})
215							}
216						}
217					}
218					return true
219				})
220			}
221		}
222	
223		if len(findings) > 0 {
224			var b strings.Builder
225			b.WriteString("non-canonical persisted-status literal(s) assigned to bead.Bead.Status:\n")
226			for _, f := range findings {
227				b.WriteString("  - ")
228				b.WriteString(f.Pos.String())
229				b.WriteString(" (")
230				b.WriteString(f.Context)
231				b.WriteString("): \"")
232				b.WriteString(f.Literal)
233				b.WriteString("\"\n")
234			}
235			b.WriteString("Allowed values are bead.CanonicalStatuses: ")
236			b.WriteString(strings.Join(canonicalSorted(), ", "))
237			b.WriteString("\nUse the typed constants (bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress, ...) where possible. ")
238			b.WriteString("If you intended to mark execution sub-state (e.g. needs_investigation), encode it as a label or event, not as a persisted status — see TD-031.")
239			t.Fatal(b.String())
240		}
241	}
242	
243	// receiverIsBead reports whether the receiver expression of `recv.Status`
244	// refers to a value or pointer of type bead.Bead.
245	func receiverIsBead(pkg *packages.Package, recv ast.Expr) bool {
246		if pkg.TypesInfo == nil {
247			return false
248		}
249		tv, ok := pkg.TypesInfo.Types[recv]
250		if !ok {
251			return false
252		}
253		return typeIsBead(tv.Type.String())
254	}
255	
256	// compositeIsBead reports whether a composite literal constructs a
257	// bead.Bead value (or pointer thereto).
258	func compositeIsBead(pkg *packages.Package, cl *ast.CompositeLit) bool {
259		if pkg.TypesInfo == nil {
260			return false
261		}
262		tv, ok := pkg.TypesInfo.Types[cl]
263		if !ok {
264			return false
265		}
266		return typeIsBead(tv.Type.String())
267	}
268	
269	// typeIsBead matches the textual rendering of types.Type.String() against
270	// the bead.Bead named type, with optional pointer prefix.
271	func typeIsBead(s string) bool {
272		s = strings.TrimPrefix(s, "*")
273		return s == "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead.Bead"
274	}
275	
276	// tdEnumPattern matches the persisted-status enumeration code-block line in
277	// TD-031 §2: `open | in_progress | closed | blocked | proposed | cancelled`.
278	var tdEnumPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^([a-z_]+(?:\s*\|\s*[a-z_]+)+)\s*$`)
279	
280	// TestTDDocMatchesSchemaEnum guards the TD-031 persisted-status section
281	// against drift from the schema enum. The TD §2 fenced code block contains
282	// a single pipe-separated enumeration line; that line must list exactly the
283	// same set of statuses as the schema.
284	func TestTDDocMatchesSchemaEnum(t *testing.T) {
285		tdPath := filepath.Join(repoRoot(t), "docs", "helix", "02-design", "technical-designs", "TD-031-bead-state-machine.md")
286		data, err := os.ReadFile(tdPath)
287		require.NoError(t, err, "read TD doc at %s", tdPath)
288	
289		// Locate §2 (Persisted Status Enumeration). The check is anchored on the
290		// section heading rather than absolute line numbers so the test stays
291		// stable under benign edits.
292		text := string(data)
293		idx := strings.Index(text, "## 2. Persisted Status Enumeration")
294		require.NotEqual(t, -1, idx, `TD-031 missing "## 2. Persisted Status Enumeration" heading at %s`, tdPath)
295		tail := text[idx:]
296		// Limit search to this section by stopping at the next "## " heading.
297		if next := strings.Index(tail[len("## 2. Persisted Status Enumeration"):], "\n## "); next != -1 {
298			tail = tail[:len("## 2. Persisted Status Enumeration")+next]
299		}
300	
301		matches := tdEnumPattern.FindStringSubmatch(tail)
302		require.NotNil(t, matches, `TD-031 §2 has no pipe-separated status enumeration line at %s — expected something like "open | in_progress | closed | blocked | proposed | cancelled"`, tdPath)
303	
304		parts := strings.Split(matches[1], "|")
305		got := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
306		for _, p := range parts {
307			got = append(got, strings.TrimSpace(p))
308		}
309		sort.Strings(got)
310	
311		want := schemaEnum(t)
312		if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, want) {
313			t.Fatalf(`TD-031 §2 persisted-status enumeration is out of sync with the JSON Schema.
314	  TD-031 §2 lists: %v
315	  Schema lists:    %v
316	Update docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md §2 to match cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json (or vice versa). Adding a status also requires an ADR-004 amendment.`, got, want)
317		}
318	}
319	
320	// TestSchemaCompatRoundTripPreservesLabelsAndEvents is the ADR-004
321	// compatibility guard. It loads a bd-export fixture that includes labels,
322	// dependencies, and events (the latter via Extra), runs it through DDx's
323	// unmarshal → marshal → unmarshal pipeline, and asserts every field —
324	// known and unknown — survives byte-equivalent.
325	func TestSchemaCompatRoundTripPreservesLabelsAndEvents(t *testing.T) {
326		fxPath := filepath.Join("testdata", "bd_export_with_labels_events.jsonl")
327		data, err := os.ReadFile(fxPath)
328		require.NoError(t, err, "read fixture %s", fxPath)
329		line := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
330		require.NotEmpty(t, line, "fixture %s is empty", fxPath)
331	
332		// Parse the fixture as a generic map so we can compare every field after
333		// the round-trip.
334		var original map[string]any
335		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &original), "fixture is not valid JSON")
336	
337		b1, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
338		require.NoError(t, err, "first unmarshal")
339	
340		out, err := marshalBead(b1)
341		require.NoError(t, err, "marshal")
342	
343		b2, err := unmarshalBead(out)
344		require.NoError(t, err, "second unmarshal")
345		assert.Equal(t, b1, b2, "Bead struct must round-trip equal")
346	
347		var roundTripped map[string]any
348		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(out, &roundTripped), "marshalled output is not valid JSON")
349	
350		// Compare every key from the original. unmarshal/marshal may normalize
351		// timestamp formatting, so compare time fields by parsed value rather
352		// than by string equality.
353		timeFields := map[string]bool{"created_at": true, "updated_at": true}
354		for k, want := range original {
355			got, ok := roundTripped[k]
356			if !ok {
357				t.Errorf("round-trip dropped field %q (value was %v) — fixture: %s", k, want, fxPath)
358				continue
359			}
360			if timeFields[k] {
361				// Compare time strings semantically.
362				gs, _ := got.(string)
363				ws, _ := want.(string)
364				if gs != ws && !sameRFC3339(gs, ws) {
365					t.Errorf("round-trip mutated time field %q: got %v, want %v", k, got, want)
366				}
367				continue
368			}
369			if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, want) {
370				t.Errorf(`round-trip mutated field %q:
371	  got:  %#v
372	  want: %#v
373	fixture: %s`, k, got, want, fxPath)
374			}
375		}
376	
377		// Spot-check that the structured fields we care about most are intact.
378		assert.Equal(t, []string{"area:bead", "kind:guardrail", "phase:2"}, b2.Labels, "labels must round-trip")
379		require.NotNil(t, b2.Extra["events"], "events (preserved-extras) must survive round-trip")
380		events, ok := b2.Extra["events"].([]any)
381		require.True(t, ok, "events must round-trip as a JSON array, got %T", b2.Extra["events"])
382		require.Len(t, events, 2, "fixture has two events; both must survive")
383	}
384	
385	// sameRFC3339 reports whether two strings represent the same instant when
386	// parsed as RFC3339 timestamps. Used so the round-trip test does not
387	// false-fail on cosmetic timestamp re-formatting (e.g. trailing nanos).
388	func sameRFC3339(a, b string) bool {
389		if a == "" || b == "" {
390			return false
391		}
392		ta, err1 := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, a)
393		if err1 != nil {
394			ta, err1 = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, a)
395		}
396		tb, err2 := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, b)
397		if err2 != nil {
398			tb, err2 = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, b)
399		}
400		if err1 != nil || err2 != nil {
401			return false
402		}
403		return ta.Equal(tb)
404	}
405

input.content:
No code changes required: ddx-861ad6a9 is already implemented and merged.

Evidence
--------
- Implementation commit: 6a005ea4 "test(bead): add 4 CI guards for state-machine drift prevention [ddx-861ad6a9]"
  Reachable from current HEAD (0e47f9ac, the pre-execute-bead checkpoint), via merge 0ca93d0a.
- Files in tree (all tracked under HEAD):
    cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go
    cli/internal/bead/testdata/bd_export_with_labels_events.jsonl
    cli/internal/bead/types.go (CanonicalStatuses)
    cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json
    docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md

Acceptance verification
-----------------------
1. Four named guard tests exist in cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go:
     - TestSchemaEnumMatchesCanonicalStatuses
     - TestStatusLiteralAudit
     - TestTDDocMatchesSchemaEnum
     - TestSchemaCompatRoundTripPreservesLabelsAndEvents
2. Each uses t.Fatalf/t.Errorf with a message naming the file paths and the
   specific drift, so a developer who breaks one is pointed at the exact
   location to fix. (The original commit message records that drift
   detection was verified by deliberately breaking each guard.)
3. Run-time check just performed in this worktree:
     cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/ -run '<all four>' -v -count=1
   Result: all four PASS in 1.31s total — well under the 5s budget.
4. The tests are plain Go tests under the standard package path; the CI
   workflow's `go test ./...` invocation picks them up with no opt-in flag.
5. The guards' purposes are explained in the file-level comment block at
   the top of sync_test.go (lines 3-28), one paragraph per guard.

Note on the bead's REVIEW:BLOCK
-------------------------------
The bead notes report "Diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no test files were added." That observation applied to an
earlier attempt; the implementation in commit 6a005ea4 (already merged)
adds the test files and they are present and passing in this worktree.
Re-running the four guards in this attempt confirms green status; no
further action is required.

Bead ddx-861ad6a9 is already satisfied by merged commit 6a005ea4. All four CI guards exist in `cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go`, all tracked in HEAD, and all pass in 1.31s:

- `TestSchemaEnumMatchesCanonicalStatuses`
- `TestStatusLiteralAudit`
- `TestTDDocMatchesSchemaEnum`
- `TestSchemaCompatRoundTripPreservesLabelsAndEvents`

The bead's REVIEW:BLOCK note ("no test files were added") was from a stale earlier attempt; the test files are present and green now. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting verification. No new commit needed.
